execution_summary: We built a Python-based Docker image (python:3.10-slim with pandas, numpy, scikit-learn, statsmodels). Inside the container we ran k17_data_prep__py.py, which detected pre-existing processed data, skipped raw merging, and launched k17_analysis__py.py. The hierarchical OLS replicated the original Model 3. No code or dependency fixes were necessary. The focal req_relax coefficient remained positive and statistically significant at the preregistered one-tailed level.

code_executed.logs: Processed data already available; analysis script executed. OLS summary printed and saved results to /app/data/replication_results.csv.
code_executed.environment: Docker container: python 3.10-slim, pandas 1.5, numpy 1.23, scikit-learn 1.2, statsmodels 0.13

results.hypothesis_tested: Higher weekend relaxation (REQ-Relax, Time 2) positively predicts joviality at the end of the subsequent work-week (PANAS-X Joviality, Time 3) controlling for baseline joviality and covariates.

tables.table_description: Full regression output saved from statsmodels.
